what_it_does: A high-fidelity desktop HTML/CSS mockup for a forensic decision-trajectory audit surface (C5) in the company's operating UI. It renders three read-only 'lenses' over the append-only event log — causal lineage, per-decision trajectory, and raw oldest-first stream — alongside a right-side pivot panel for other decisions and sessions.
observations: The file is a self-contained HTML/CSS prototype with no JavaScript.
A large head comment documents real backend grounding (suite.py, store/fs_store.py, bridge.py line references), registered ui:// addresses, and proposed-but-unregistered addresses.
The main canvas is organized into three register cards: (1) derived_from causal lineage, (2) decision_view per-sid trajectory, (3) replay raw stream.
It explicitly marks proposed vs existing UI addresses and backend endpoints with inline prose and a .proposed chip style.
It includes an 'audit window' honesty note about the 999-event truncation limit of decision_view versus the full-tail reads used by session_view and replay.
It uses design-system.css variables and Bricolage Grotesque / IBM Plex Mono typography.
Many elements carry data-ui-ref attributes linking visual items to conceptual ui:// addresses.
The right panel provides tabs and a list of auditable decisions/sessions with verdict tags (approve/decide/reject).
It declares the surface as operator-directed and forensic, not part of a triage journey.
standouts: The head comment functions as both design rationale and a code-grounding ledger.
The three-register model (causal lineage / decision trajectory / raw stream) is the central organizing idea.
Honest depiction of a real backend read-window limitation directly in the UI prose.
Extensive data-ui-ref annotations that wire the mockup to conceptual addresses.
conventions: Follows the company's app shell grid areas (top, rail, canvas, panel, foot).
Uses CSS custom properties from design-system.css.
Uses semantic class naming and data-ui-ref annotations.
Breaks convention by embedding a large explanatory comment block in the HTML head rather than a separate design doc.
Uses inline styles in a few places for one-off proposed-branch notes.
concerns: Some data-ui-ref values appear inconsistent or possibly stale (e.g., a button references ui://inbox/replay while the raw stream lens is registered as ui://activity/replay).
The mockup mixes real backend line numbers, registered addresses, and proposed addresses, creating drift risk if code changes.
No JavaScript or accessibility attributes (aria labels, roles) for what is presented as an interactive audit surface.
Inline styles and repeated font-family declarations reduce maintainability.
notes: A forensic, read-only audit mockup that grounds every UI element in real event-log semantics and proposed ui:// addresses.
questions: Which ui:// address owns the whole-path replay lens — ui://activity/replay or ui://inbox/coa/replay?
Is the right-panel 'Audit' tab a new surface or a mode of the existing C-area panel?
How does the operator navigate from a decision in the inbox to this audit view?
Are the event kind colors and field whitelist in the CSS aligned with the actual event taxonomy?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches — the file states it is a DRAFT probe for a forensic decision-trajectory audit and then builds exactly that UI.
apparent_themes: Read-only audit surfaces that expose backend causality.
Honest representation of system limits in the UI.
Address-driven UI design via ui:// references.
intention_signals: It is trying to make the system's decision-making legible after the fact by binding every system act back to the operator verdict that authorized it, using the event log as the single source of truth.
novelty: The explicit three-register model that separates causal lineage, per-decision trajectory, and raw stream, and the practice of annotating a mockup with both real code references and proposed ui:// addresses.
contribution: A concrete visual and structural proposal for how to surface decision causality and event-log auditability in the operating UI, including an honesty pattern for communicating read-window limits.
summary_for_embedding: Desktop HTML mockup of C5, a forensic decision-trajectory audit surface showing causal lineage, per-decision trajectory, and raw event stream over the append-only log.
intention_for_embedding: To prototype a read-only audit view that lets operators trace how a decision was proposed, resolved, and authorized downstream system acts.
